steps.text: Masking replaces sensitive values in tool responses with stable aliases. The AI sees the aliases; OpenMaskit swaps them back to real values when the agent sends them in a subsequent call.
Easiest path: in the response tree above, hover any leaf value and click 'Mask'. That creates a path-based rule for that field — future responses will be masked automatically.

For values that don't sit at a fixed field path — API keys, IPs, emails scattered through text — use a regex instead. Example: '\b[A-Z0-9]{32}\b' for 32-char API keys, '\b\d{1,3}(\.\d{1,3}){3}\b' for IPv4.
Set an alias prefix like 'api_key' to generate readable aliases: 'api_key_1', 'api_key_2', etc.
Click 'Preview' to test your regex on the actual response. You'll see exactly what gets masked, and a 'Save Mapper' button appears — click that to apply the rule to all future responses.
